seo.title: E-Commerce Research Agents AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for research agents AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: E-Commerce research agents that autonomously gather and synthesize product, pricing, and policy information carry significant compliance risk under FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, and PCI DSS frameworks. Errors in product claims, pricing accuracy, or return policy representation can trigger regulatory enforcement, customer disputes, and direct revenue loss. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls for teams deploying multi-step web search and synthesis agents in e-commerce environments.

sections.heading: Source Verification & Data Integrity
sections.description: Ensure the research agent only ingests and synthesizes information from verified, authoritative sources to prevent propagation of inaccurate product claims or pricing data.

items.label: Allowlist approved data sources
items.description: Maintain an explicit allowlist of approved domains (e.g., manufacturer sites, official retailer pages, government databases) and configure the agent to reject or flag content from unlisted sources before synthesis.

items.label: Implement source credibility scoring
items.description: Assign credibility scores to each source based on domain authority, publication date, and known accuracy history; require a minimum score threshold before including data in agent output.

items.label: Cross-reference product claims across at least two independent sources
items.description: Configure the agent to require corroboration from at least two independent, authoritative sources before surfacing any product specification, ingredient claim, or performance assertion to end users.

items.label: Log all retrieved URLs and page snapshots per research session
items.description: Store a timestamped record of every URL fetched and the page content snapshot used during each research session to support audit trails for FTC substantiation requirements.

items.label: Detect and exclude user-generated content as primary evidence
items.description: Implement classifiers to identify forums, social media posts, and unverified reviews, and prevent the agent from treating them as primary substantiation for product claims.

items.label: Validate that pricing data is fetched in real time from the merchant's own system
items.description: Ensure pricing information displayed to customers is pulled directly from the live product catalog or pricing API, not cached or scraped from third-party pages, to maintain accuracy compliance.

items.label: Flag conflicting information across sources for human review
items.description: When the agent detects contradictory claims (e.g., different prices or incompatible product specs across sources), route the output to a human reviewer queue rather than auto-resolving with a single source.

sections.heading: Claim Accuracy & FTC Compliance
sections.description: All product claims, promotional statements, and comparative assertions generated or surfaced by the research agent must meet FTC substantiation standards and avoid deceptive or misleading representations.

items.label: Run all product claims through a substantiation checklist before display
items.description: Before surfacing any efficacy, performance, or comparative claim to a user, the agent must verify that supporting evidence exists in the retrieved sources and meets FTC's 'competent and reliable scientific evidence' standard where applicable.

items.label: Prohibit superlatives without documented evidence
items.description: Configure output filters to block or flag phrases like 'best,' 'fastest,' or 'most effective' unless the agent has retrieved a verifiable, dated third-party study or ranking that substantiates the claim.

items.label: Disclose AI-generated summaries to end users
items.description: Add a visible, plain-language disclosure on all agent-generated product summaries and comparisons stating that the content was compiled by an AI research tool and may not reflect the most current information.

items.label: Implement a bias detection pass for comparative product research
items.description: After synthesis, run a secondary model pass or rule-based check to detect if the agent's output disproportionately favors high-margin or sponsored products without factual basis, and flag for review.

items.label: Enforce recency thresholds on claim evidence
items.description: Reject or flag any source older than a configurable threshold (e.g., 12 months for pricing, 24 months for product specs) to prevent the agent from presenting outdated information as current fact.

items.label: Validate return and refund policy representations against live policy documents
items.description: When the agent summarizes return or refund policies, it must fetch the current policy directly from the merchant's official policy page and compare it against any cached or synthesized version before presenting to the user.

items.label: Log all claim-to-source mappings for post-hoc audit
items.description: Maintain a structured log that maps each claim in the agent's output to the specific source URL, retrieved text snippet, and timestamp, enabling rapid response to FTC inquiries or customer disputes.

items.label: Block health or safety claims that require regulatory approval
items.description: Implement keyword and semantic filters to prevent the agent from generating or surfacing drug, medical device, or health benefit claims that require FDA or equivalent regulatory substantiation without explicit human approval.

sections.heading: Privacy, Data Handling & Consumer Rights
sections.description: Research agents that process user queries, browsing behavior, or purchase history must comply with CCPA and GDPR data minimization, consent, and deletion requirements.

items.label: Classify and minimize personal data used in research queries
items.description: Audit what user data (e.g., search history, location, purchase behavior) is passed to the research agent; strip or pseudonymize any PII not strictly necessary for the research task before the agent processes it.

items.label: Obtain explicit consent before using browsing or purchase history to personalize research results
items.description: Implement a consent gate that requires users to affirmatively opt in before the agent uses their behavioral data to tailor product research outputs, with a clear explanation of how the data will be used.

items.label: Implement data retention limits on agent session logs
items.description: Define and enforce a maximum retention period for research session logs (e.g., 90 days) and configure automated deletion pipelines to comply with CCPA and GDPR storage limitation principles.

items.label: Support user data deletion requests that include agent-generated profiles
items.description: Ensure your CCPA/GDPR deletion workflow covers any user preference profiles or behavioral models built by the research agent, not just transactional records in the primary database.

items.label: Restrict agent access to payment card data and enforce PCI DSS scope boundaries
items.description: Ensure the research agent has no access to raw payment card numbers, CVVs, or full PANs; enforce network segmentation so the agent operates entirely outside PCI DSS cardholder data environment (CDE) scope.

items.label: Document third-party data processors used by the agent in your privacy notice
items.description: Identify all external APIs, search engines, and data providers the agent calls during research, and ensure they are listed as sub-processors in your GDPR Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and disclosed in your privacy policy.

items.label: Provide users with a mechanism to opt out of AI-assisted research personalization
items.description: Implement a clearly labeled opt-out control in account settings that disables behavioral personalization for research agent outputs, and honor opt-out signals within 15 business days per CCPA requirements.

sections.heading: Accessibility, Monitoring & Incident Response
sections.description: Research agent outputs must be accessible to all users under ADA guidelines, and the system must include real-time monitoring and a defined incident response process for compliance failures.

items.label: Ensure all agent-generated content meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards
items.description: Test agent output rendering (product summaries, comparison tables, policy excerpts) against WCAG 2.1 AA criteria including sufficient color contrast, screen reader compatibility, and keyboard navigability before production deployment.

items.label: Provide text alternatives for all agent-generated visual content
items.description: If the research agent generates charts, infographics, or image-based comparisons, ensure alt text or a text-based equivalent is automatically generated and attached to every visual output.

items.label: Deploy real-time monitoring for pricing accuracy drift
items.description: Implement automated alerts that trigger within 15 minutes if the agent surfaces a price that deviates more than a defined threshold (e.g., 5%) from the live catalog price, routing the discrepancy to an on-call operations team.

items.label: Establish a defined SLA for remediating inaccurate agent outputs reported by customers
items.description: Define and document a maximum response time (e.g., 4 business hours) for investigating and correcting agent-generated product or pricing errors reported via customer support, and track SLA adherence monthly.

items.label: Conduct quarterly red-team exercises targeting agent output manipulation
items.description: Schedule quarterly adversarial testing sessions where internal teams attempt to manipulate the agent into surfacing false claims, inflated prices, or fabricated return policies via prompt injection or poisoned search results.

items.label: Maintain a human-in-the-loop escalation path for high-stakes research outputs
items.description: Define categories of high-stakes outputs (e.g., health product claims, pricing above a dollar threshold, legal policy interpretations) that require human review and approval before being displayed to customers.

items.label: Create and test an incident response runbook specific to agent compliance failures
items.description: Document step-by-step procedures for responding to agent-caused compliance incidents (e.g., FTC-triggering false claims, CCPA breach, pricing errors at scale), including rollback procedures, customer notification templates, and regulatory reporting timelines.

items.label: Track and report agent accuracy KPIs to compliance leadership monthly
items.description: Define measurable KPIs (e.g., claim substantiation rate, pricing accuracy rate, source recency compliance rate) and deliver a monthly dashboard to compliance and legal leadership to support ongoing governance.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: FTC Endorsement & Testimonial Guides (16 CFR Part 255) require that any product claim surfaced or synthesized by the agent be substantiated by competent evidence at the time it is made; AI-generated summaries that aggregate unverified user reviews or cherry-pick positive claims without disclosure may constitute deceptive advertising and expose the operator to civil penalties.

CCPA (Cal. Civ. Code §1798.100 et seq.) and GDPR (Article 22) both impose restrictions on automated decision-making that produces legal or similarly significant effects on consumers; if the research agent personalizes product recommendations based on inferred sensitive categories (e.g., health conditions, financial status), additional opt-in consent and impact assessments are required before deployment.

PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.8 mandates that merchants maintain a register of all third-party service providers with access to cardholder data and confirm their PCI compliance annually; any API or data pipeline connected to the research agent that touches payment-adjacent data must be scoped, assessed, and documented accordingly.

ADA Title III and the DOJ's 2024 web accessibility rule (28 CFR Part 36) establish that e-commerce websites are places of public accommodation; agent-generated content that is not accessible to users with disabilities (e.g., screen-reader-incompatible comparison tables, inaccessible CAPTCHA flows triggered by agent activity) creates litigation exposure under ADA and analogous state laws such as California's Unruh Civil Rights Act.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your research agents against FTC, CCPA, and PCI DSS requirements—catching pricing errors, unsubstantiated claims, and data handling violations before they reach your customers.
